calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-15-2020 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-02-2019 Set For Order To Show Cause On Feb-04-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-06-2019 Set For Case Management Conference On Dec-18-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant City And County Of San Franciscos Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Aug-06-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kelly Collins, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel: 415.554.3914, for defendant and moving party;
Matthew Martin, Tel: 415.225.3827, plaintiff and opposing party, appearing on his own behalf.

RULING: The matter was argued. The Court informed the plaintiff that filing a claim against the city is a prerequisite to filing a complaint. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT as follows:

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's (sued as San Francisco General Hospital) motion for summary judgment is GRANTED. The undisputed evidence establishes that plaintiff never filed a government claim with the City. (Rothschild Decl., Para. 5.) The filing of such a claim "is more than a procedural requirement, it is a condition precedent to plaintiff's maintaining an action against defendant, in short, an integral part of plaintiff's cause of action." (State of California v. Superior Court (2004) 32 Cal.4th 1234, 1240 (internal quotations omitted).) Because plaintiff failed to file the necessary claim, his action against the City fails as a matter of law.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant City And County Of San Franciscos Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jul-02-2019 Continued To Aug-06-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Matthew Clyde Martin, 415-225-3827 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Renee Rosenblit, Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-3800 of the Office of the City Attorney for Defendant CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court announces Plaintiff did not oppose the motion, did not contest the tentative ruling, informs him of the Administrative process, tells Plaintiff to provide Defendant's Counsel his address on the record and to file Opposition papers. Plaintiff wishes to go to trial directly.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Continued to August 6, 2019 at 9:30 am. Plaintiff's Opposition Papers filed/due by July 23, 2019, Courtesy copies to the Dept. City may file a reply on statutory schedule if it needs to. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
